_description: Railway production service topology for the Fincept Terminal. Railway does not natively parse a multi-service JSON config the way docker-compose does; this file is operator documentation that mirrors the service graph to create in the Railway dashboard. Pair with docs/RAILWAY_DEPLOY_GUIDE.md. Paper-only trading is enforced (FINCEPT_TRADING_MODE=paper, FINCEPT_OMS_ROUTER=sim). No real secrets are stored here — every secret is a ${{secrets.NAME}} placeholder to be set in the Railway dashboard.
_safety_invariants: No broker credentials (ALPACA_API_KEY / ALPACA_API_SECRET) are set on Railway
All secrets use ${{secrets.NAME}} placeholders — set them in the Railway dashboard, never in the repo
RunPod GPU workloads run externally; Railway hosts the API + Dashboard control plane only
RunPod env vars use canonical RUNPOD_* names (RUNPOD_API_KEY, RUNPOD_TRAINING_ENDPOINT_ID, RUNPOD_INFERENCE_ENDPOINT_ID); deprecated QUANT_FOUNDRY_RUNPOD_* names are read as fallbacks with a warning
No production RunPod endpoint IDs are committed to the repo — they are ${{secrets.*}} placeholders set in the Railway dashboard
QUANT_FOUNDRY_CALLBACK_SECRET must be identical on Railway and RunPod; verify with GET /quant-foundry/health/runpod-canary after deploy

postgres.notes: Provision via Railway dashboard (Add → Database → PostgreSQL). The connection string is exposed as the service's DATABASE_URL variable. Wire it into the API service as FINCEPT_DB_URL. Hobby plan (~1GB) is sufficient for v1; upgrade to a larger plan when artifact rows exceed ~500k.

redis.notes: Provision via Railway dashboard (Add → Database → Redis). The connection string is exposed as the service's REDIS_URL variable. Wire it into the API service as FINCEPT_REDIS_URL. Used for the shared Redis client opened in api.main.lifespan, the AlpacaScheduler, NewsScheduler, and the api heartbeat.

object-storage.notes: Provision via Railway dashboard (Add → Object Storage). S3-compatible. Railway exposes ENDPOINT, ACCESS_KEY, SECRET_KEY, and a default bucket region. Create a bucket named 'fincept-artifacts' for model weights, training receipts, dossiers, and shadow-ledger snapshots.

volumes./data: persistent volume for quant foundry durable stores (outbox, inbox, shadow ledger, dossier registry). Mount before first deploy so the lifespan-created gateway has a writable base dir.

api.notes: The FastAPI lifespan (services/api/src/api/main.py) starts four background poll tasks when QUANT_FOUNDRY_ENABLED=true and mode is runpod_shadow: RunPod result poll (15s), settlement sweep (60s), tournament sweep (300s), and shadow dispatch (300s). The /health endpoint is public (no auth) so Railway's healthcheck can probe it.

dashboard.notes: Next.js dashboard. NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL must be the API service's public Railway domain (https://<api>.up.railway.app) so the browser can reach the API. If a custom domain is configured, point this at the custom domain instead.

_secret_injection.description: Secrets are NOT stored in this file or in the repo. Set each ${{secrets.NAME}} placeholder in the Railway dashboard under the API service's Variables tab (mark as secret so it is masked in logs).

secrets.name: CALLBACK_SECRET
secrets.purpose: HMAC secret validating RunPod → API callback requests (quant_foundry callback endpoint). Must be identical on both the Railway API and the RunPod endpoint templates. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
secrets.where_to_set: Railway dashboard → API service → Variables → New Variable → mark as Secret. Also set in RunPod endpoint template env as QUANT_FOUNDRY_CALLBACK_SECRET.

secrets.name: RUNPOD_API_KEY
secrets.purpose: RunPod API key for dispatching training/inference jobs from the API to the RunPod endpoints. Obtain from the RunPod dashboard.
secrets.where_to_set: Railway dashboard → API service → Variables → New Variable → mark as Secret

secrets.name: RUNPOD_TRAINING_ENDPOINT_ID
secrets.purpose: RunPod serverless endpoint ID for the training worker. Obtain from the RunPod dashboard (Endpoints page). Do not commit production endpoint IDs to the repo.
secrets.where_to_set: Railway dashboard → API service → Variables → New Variable

secrets.name: RUNPOD_INFERENCE_ENDPOINT_ID
secrets.purpose: RunPod serverless endpoint ID for the inference worker. Obtain from the RunPod dashboard (Endpoints page). Do not commit production endpoint IDs to the repo.
secrets.where_to_set: Railway dashboard → API service → Variables → New Variable

never_on_railway: FINCEPT_ALPACA_API_KEY — broker credentials stay off Railway (paper-only, OMS_ROUTER=sim)
FINCEPT_ALPACA_API_SECRET — broker credentials stay off Railway
FINCEPT_BINANCE_API_SECRET — exchange secrets stay off Railway

_cross_references: railway.json — staging config (READ ONLY; this file is the production counterpart)
docs/RAILWAY_DEPLOY_GUIDE.md — step-by-step deployment + cost comparison with AWS
